feed.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms

entry.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
entry.summary:
The discourse revolves around the challenges and hopes within the Bitcoin community concerning the development and implementation of post-quantum (PQ) zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs). The anticipation is that, with time, at least one PQ ZKP will emerge that is broadly recognized for its quality, especially as the advent of cryptographically relevant quantum computers (CRQC) appears imminent. This advancement is seen as crucial, and there's a suggestion that perhaps the Bitcoin community should consider funding such developments in the short term to ensure readiness.
The conversation also touches on the practical aspects of Bitcoin usage and the evolution of wallet services, highlighting the case of Trust wallet. Initially, Trust wallet used fresh addresses for transactions, a practice aimed at enhancing user privacy and security. However, due to the confusion it caused among users, this feature was made optional and eventually removed entirely, as it saw minimal engagement from the user base. This anecdote serves as an illustration of the broader challenges faced by the Bitcoin ecosystem in balancing technological advancements with user friendliness and adoption.
Overall, the exchange underscores a tension within the Bitcoin community between optimism for future technological solutions that enhance security and privacy and realism about the current state of user engagement and technological adoption. It reflects a keen awareness of the need for continued investment in innovation, alongside an acknowledgment of the hurdles in making such technologies accessible and adopted by the wider user base.
